title: Bayesian Methods for Subgroup Efficacy and Safety: Application to Japanese Patients in JAVELIN Renal 101.
abstract: JAVELIN Renal 101 revealed that avelumab plus axitinib improved progression-free survival (PFS) compared with sunitinib in advanced renal cell carcinoma. However, Japanese subgroup estimates were imprecise. In this study, we evaluated how Bayesian borrowing assumptions translate into posterior probabilities for this subgroup.
